subject: UX & Research
title: Fisher and Randomization
description: A 60-second lesson on Fisher and Randomization.

hook.text: A woman claimed she could taste whether tea or milk was poured first. Ronald Fisher designed a test to find out.

buildup.text: Fisher realized you need randomization to eliminate bias. His 1935 book formalized experimental design.

discovery.text: Random assignment ensures groups are comparable. Without it, differences could come from the groups, not the treatment.

twist.text: Many A/B tests skip proper randomization. They split by geography or time, adding bias they can't measure.

climax.text: Fisher's insight powers every modern A/B test: randomly assign users so the groups differ only in the change you made.

punchline.text: Random assignment removes hidden bias. Fisher proved it with tea.

quiz.question: Why is random assignment essential in A/B testing?
options: It ensures groups are comparable so differences come from the change, not the groups
Randomization is required by law in most countries
